market.title: One name at a time: How Die Zeit built a searchable database of Nazi Party members
market.summary_raw: In the final days of World War II, as the German Reich collapsed, Nazi officials ordered millions of party membership cards to be destroyed. The vast card index that documented membership across Germany survived largely because a paper mill operator, Hanns Huber, chose to hand the records over to the advancing US forces rather than...

market.title: In Ethiopia, fact-checking can be a matter of life and death
market.summary_raw: As reports of drone strikes and fresh clashes between Ethiopian government forces and Tigrayan fighters surfaced in late January, fears of a return to full-scale war spread quickly.
